# UC-10.16.8 · Endpoint Protection Coverage Trending

## Description

Percentage of endpoints with current antivirus or EDR definitions and healthy agent status demonstrates control effectiveness for audits and cyber insurance. Downward trends often track OS rebuilds, mergers, or agent failures rather than policy alone.

## Value

Percentage of endpoints with current antivirus or EDR definitions and healthy agent status demonstrates control effectiveness for audits and cyber insurance. Downward trends often track OS rebuilds, mergers, or agent failures rather than policy alone.

## Implementation

Map vendor-specific “last update” and protection-state fields; some use epoch seconds. Join to CMDB or ES `assets` to exclude decommissioned hosts from the denominator. Alert when coverage drops more than a few points week over week. Document exceptions (OT, lab) via a lookup to avoid false remediation tickets.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for CrowdStrike, Microsoft Defender TA, Tanium, Splunk Enterprise Security (asset framework).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=main` or `index=endpoint` EDR inventory, `sourcetype=stash` weekly compliance rollups.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Map vendor-specific “last update” and protection-state fields; some use epoch seconds. Join to CMDB or ES `assets` to exclude decommissioned hosts from the denominator. Alert when coverage drops more than a few points week over week. Document exceptions (OT, lab) via a lookup to avoid false remediation tickets.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=main (sourcetype="crowdstrike:hosts" OR sourcetype="ms:defender:machineinfo" OR sourcetype="tanium:endpoint")
| eval defs_age_hours=(now()-strptime(last_av_update,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"))/3600
| eval current=if(isnotnull(defs_age_hours) AND defs_age_hours<=48 AND match(status,"normal|active|enabled"),1,0)
| bin _time span=1w
| stats dc(host) as fleet, sum(current) as compliant by _time
| eval coverage_pct=round(100*compliant/nullif(fleet,0),2)
| timechart span=1w first(coverage_pct) as av_edr_coverage_pct
```

Understanding this SPL

**Endpoint Protection Coverage Trending** — Percentage of endpoints with current antivirus or EDR definitions and healthy agent status demonstrates control effectiveness for audits and cyber insurance. Downward trends often track OS rebuilds, mergers, or agent failures rather than policy alone.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=main` or `index=endpoint` EDR inventory, `sourcetype=stash` weekly compliance rollups. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for CrowdStrike, Microsoft Defender TA, Tanium, Splunk Enterprise Security (asset framework). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: main; **sourcetype**: crowdstrike:hosts, ms:defender:machineinfo, tanium:endpoint. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=main, sourcetype="crowdstrike:hosts". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **defs_age_hours** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **current** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by _time** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **coverage_pct** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=1w** buckets — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.

Step 3 — Validate
For the denominator, compare to **Active Directory, CMDB, or your EDR’s** “last seen” list so decommissioned boxes are not unfairly punishing the percentage.



Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (coverage percentage over time), single value (current vs target), pie chart (compliant vs noncompliant).


## SPL

```spl
index=main (sourcetype="crowdstrike:hosts" OR sourcetype="ms:defender:machineinfo" OR sourcetype="tanium:endpoint")
| eval defs_age_hours=(now()-strptime(last_av_update,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"))/3600
| eval current=if(isnotnull(defs_age_hours) AND defs_age_hours<=48 AND match(status,"normal|active|enabled"),1,0)
| bin _time span=1w
| stats dc(host) as fleet, sum(current) as compliant by _time
| eval coverage_pct=round(100*compliant/nullif(fleet,0),2)
| timechart span=1w first(coverage_pct) as av_edr_coverage_pct
```

## Visualization

Line chart (coverage percentage over time), single value (current vs target), pie chart (compliant vs noncompliant).
